verses.text: When Abner dies, Ish-bosheth loses heart and all Israel is troubled, for the prop is gone. Two of his own captains, Rechab and Baanah, sense the kingdom crumbling. When a cause rests on one strong man, his fall leaves the rest grasping for their own advantage.

When Abner dies, Ish-bosheth loses heart and all Israel is troubled, for the prop is gone. Two of his own captains, Rechab and Baanah, sense the kingdom crumbling. When a cause rests on one strong man, his fall leaves the rest grasping for their own advantage.

When Abner dies, Ish-bosheth loses heart and all Israel is troubled, for the prop is gone. Two of his own captains, Rechab and Baanah, sense the kingdom crumbling. When a cause rests on one strong man, his fall leaves the rest grasping for their own advantage.

Scripture pauses to note Jonathan's son Mephibosheth, lame in both feet since a fall in childhood when news of Saul's death came. The last hope of Saul's house is a crippled boy. Here, set quietly aside, is the one David will later seek out to show kindness for Jonathan's sake.

Rechab and Baanah slip into Ish-bosheth's house at noon and murder him as he rests on his bed, then carry off his head. They strike a helpless man asleep — the lowest kind of cowardice. Treachery against the defenceless is among the basest of all crimes.

Rechab and Baanah slip into Ish-bosheth's house at noon and murder him as he rests on his bed, then carry off his head. They strike a helpless man asleep — the lowest kind of cowardice. Treachery against the defenceless is among the basest of all crimes.

Rechab and Baanah slip into Ish-bosheth's house at noon and murder him as he rests on his bed, then carry off his head. They strike a helpless man asleep — the lowest kind of cowardice. Treachery against the defenceless is among the basest of all crimes.

They bring the head to David, expecting praise for clearing his path to the throne. But David, who would not even kill Saul, has them executed for murdering 'a righteous person in his own house.' He will not build his kingdom on innocent blood, nor reward those who do evil in his name.
